question.text: Are audit and assurance policies, procedures, and standards established, documented, approved, communicated, applied, evaluated, and maintained?

payload.draftText: Yes. BNY Mellon's management, including the Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer, with participation by the members of the Disclosure Committee, has responsibility for ensuring that there is an adequate and effective process for establishing, maintaining, and evaluating disclosure controls and procedures designed to ensure that information required to be disclosed in SEC reports is timely recorded, processed, summarized, and reported, and that such information is accumulated and communicated to management to allow timely decisions regarding required disclosure [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001390777-23-000033::Item_16#0009]. These responsibilities are likewise reflected in prior periodic filings, evidencing that the framework is documented, applied, and consistently maintained over time [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001390777-22-000043::Item_16#0006]. With respect to external audit governance, the Audit Committee has established pre-approval policies and procedures applicable to all services provided by our independent registered public accountants, consistent with SEC rules, using both class pre-approval and specific pre-approval approaches for audit and permitted non-audit services [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001193125-25-046216::Item_3#0154]. Collectively, these arrangements demonstrate that audit and assurance policies, procedures, and standards are established, documented, approved, communicated, applied, evaluated, and maintained.

questions.text: Are independent audit and assurance assessments conducted according to relevant standards at least annually?
questions.outboundPreview: Yes. BNY's Audit Committee oversees a comprehensive system of internal controls and is directly responsible for the selection, appointment, compensation, retention and oversight of the independent registered public accountants engaged to perform audit, review or attestation services, and for annuall

question.text: Are independent audit and assurance assessments conducted according to relevant standards at least annually?

payload.draftText: Yes. BNY's Audit Committee oversees a comprehensive system of internal controls and is directly responsible for the selection, appointment, compensation, retention and oversight of the independent registered public accountants engaged to perform audit, review or attestation services, and for annually evaluating KPMG, including its qualifications and independence [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001193125-24-051895::Item_5#0151]. On an annual basis, the Audit Committee reviews and discusses with management the audited financial statements and management's assessment of internal control over financial reporting, and KPMG issues its report on the financial statements and the operating effectiveness of internal control over financial reporting [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001193125-24-051895::Item_5#0153]. KPMG is an independent public accounting firm registered with the PCAOB and conducts its audit in accordance with PCAOB auditing standards, including those governing Communications with Audit Committees and auditor independence [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001193125-25-046216::Item_3#0152]. The Audit Committee's annual review of KPMG also considers KPMG's continued independence, its understanding of the financial services industry and BNY's business, and the professionalism, objectivity and integrity of the engagement team, supported by management's annual assessment of the firm [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001193125-25-046216::Item_3#0150]. These responsibilities, including annual evaluation of the external auditor, are formally assigned to the Audit Committee [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001193125-22-059759::Item_4#0140].

questions.text: Has your regulator raised any material issues in respect of your banking, custody and client money activities within the last 12 months?
questions.outboundPreview: BNY and its subsidiaries are routinely named as defendants in or made parties to pending and potential legal actions in the ordinary course of business [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001390777-25-000118::PART_II.Item_6#0168]. question.text: Has your regulator raised any material issues in respect of your banking, custody and client money activities within the last 12 months?

payload.draftText: BNY and its subsidiaries are routinely named as defendants in or made parties to pending and potential legal actions in the ordinary course of business [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001390777-25-000118::PART_II.Item_6#0168]. The firm is also subject to governmental and regulatory examinations, information-gathering requests, investigations and proceedings, both formal and informal, and claims for significant monetary damages are often asserted in many of these legal actions, while claims for disgorgement, restitution, penalties and/or other remedial actions or sanctions may be sought in governmental and regulatory matters [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001390777-24-000133::PART_II.Item_6#0174]. It is inherently difficult to predict the eventual outcomes of such matters given their complexity and the particular facts and circumstances at issue in each matter; however, on the basis of current knowledge and understanding, BNY does not believe that judgments, settlements or orders, if any, arising from these matters — either individually or in the aggregate, after giving effect to applicable reserves and insurance coverage — will have a material adverse effect [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001390777-24-000133::PART_II.Item_6#0174]. As of March 31, 2026, BNY did not record any material liabilities under its clearing and settlement exchange sponsorship arrangements [span:edgar:bny-mellon-corp:0001390777-26-000060::PART_II.Item_6#0145].
